name: sidecar-ai
description: Autonomous AI agent for coding — run full agent loops with local Ollama, Anthropic Claude, or OpenAI models

sidecar.baseUrl.markdownDescription:
Base URL for the active LLM backend.

| Backend | URL |
|---|---|
| Ollama (local) | `http://localhost:11434` |
| Anthropic | `https://api.anthropic.com` |
| OpenAI | `https://api.openai.com` |
| Kickstand *(coming soon)* | `http://localhost:11435` |

Switch backends the easy way: `SideCar: Switch Backend` in the Command Palette.

sidecar.apiKey.markdownDescription:
API key for the active backend. **Stored in VS Code SecretStorage** — never in `settings.json`. Run `SideCar: Set / Refresh API Key` to paste or rotate your key securely. Plaintext values in this setting are migrated to SecretStorage on activation and then cleared.

Ignored for local Ollama; required for Anthropic and OpenAI.

sidecar.model.markdownDescription:
Model to use for chat. Examples:

- Ollama: `qwen3-coder:30b`, `deepseek-coder:33b`, `llama3.2:latest`
- Anthropic: `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `claude-haiku-4-5`
- OpenAI: `gpt-5`, `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4o-mini`

Pick keyboard-first with `SideCar: Select Model`.

enumDescriptions: Detect from the base URL: localhost:11434 → Ollama, anthropic.com → Anthropic, localhost:11435 → Kickstand (coming soon), anything else → OpenAI-compatible.
Local Ollama (http://localhost:11434). Free, private, no key needed.
Anthropic Claude API. Requires an API key stored via `SideCar: Set / Refresh API Key`.
OpenAI-compatible API (OpenAI, LM Studio, vLLM, llama.cpp, OpenRouter, together.ai). Requires an API key.

Kickstand self-hosted LLM server (http://localhost:11435). Coming soon — not yet officially released; the backend adapter ships today for anyone running a local dev build. Reads its token from `~/.config/kickstand/token`.
sidecar.provider.markdownDescription: Which backend adapter to use when talking to the LLM. Leave as `auto` unless you're running an OpenAI-compatible server at a non-standard URL that the auto-detector can't recognize.

sidecar.systemPrompt.markdownDescription: System prompt override. Leave empty to use SideCar's built-in prompt (recommended). Custom prompts compose on top of the base; they don't replace it.

sidecar.includeWorkspace.markdownDescription: Automatically include workspace files in chat context. When off, only files you explicitly reference with `@file:path` or pin with `@pin:path` are sent to the model.

sidecar.filePatterns.markdownDescription: Glob patterns for files eligible to be included as workspace context. Applied before `sidecar.maxFiles` ranks them by relevance. Patterns use standard VS Code globbing (`**`, `*`, `?`).

sidecar.maxFiles.markdownDescription: Maximum number of workspace files included as context on each turn. Lower this if you're hitting context-limit warnings on a small local model; raise it for frontier models with larger windows.

sidecar.contextLimit.markdownDescription: Override the context token limit for local models. `0` = auto-detect with a 16K default cap. Increase if you have enough VRAM and want longer conversations. Ignored for paid backends — their limits come from the API.

sidecar.customTools.description: Custom tools that run shell commands. Format: [{ "name": "...", "description": "...", "command": "..." }]

sidecar.customModes.description: Custom agent modes with dedicated system prompts and approval behavior. Example: [{ "name": "architect", "description": "Design-only mode", "systemPrompt": "Focus on architecture...", "approvalBehavior": "manual" }]

description.description: Short description shown in the mode dropdown

systemPrompt.description: Additional system prompt text injected when this mode is active

approvalBehavior.description: Tool approval behavior for this mode

toolPermissions.description: Per-tool permission overrides for this mode

sidecar.hooks.description: Pre/post-execution hooks by tool name. Keys are tool names or '*' for all. Value: { "pre": "command", "post": "command" }

sidecar.scheduledTasks.description: Recurring tasks to run on interval

sidecar.mcpServers.markdownDescription:
MCP servers to connect to. Supports stdio, HTTP, and SSE transports. Also reads `.mcp.json` at workspace root.

**stdio:** `{ "command": "npx", "args": ["server"] }`

**HTTP:** `{ "type": "http", "url": "https://...", "headers": {} }`

**Options:** `tools` (per-tool enable/disable), `maxResultChars` (output limit, default 50000)

sidecar.agentMode.markdownDescription:
How much autonomy the agent has. Built-in modes:

- `cautious` — asks before destructive tools (default)
- `autonomous` — runs every allowed tool without asking
- `manual` — asks before every tool
- `plan` — generates a plan for your approval, then executes
- `review` — queues file edits for review; nothing hits disk

Custom modes defined in `#sidecar.customModes#` are also valid here.

sidecar.agentTemperature.markdownDescription:
Temperature for agent tool-calling requests.

- `0.1-0.3` — deterministic tool selection (recommended for agent loops)
- `0.7-1.0` — creative, more exploration

Non-tool requests always use `0`.

sidecar.agentMaxIterations.markdownDescription: Maximum number of agent loop iterations before the agent stops. Each iteration is one model call plus any tools it chose. Hitting this limit usually means the agent is stuck in a cycle — inspect the chat and narrow the task.

sidecar.agentMaxMessages.markdownDescription: Soft ceiling on conversation message count before the agent should wrap up. The agent still completes the current tool cycle if it's in the middle of one.

sidecar.agentMaxTokens.markdownDescription: Maximum total tokens consumed by a single agent run before it stops. Prevents runaway loops on paid backends. `100000` is a safe default for Claude; bump higher for frontier models with large context windows.

sidecar.requestTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for each LLM request. If no tokens arrive within this time the request is aborted. Set to 0 to disable.

sidecar.shellTimeout.description: Default timeout for shell commands in seconds

sidecar.shellMaxOutputMB.description: Maximum shell output size in MB before truncation

sidecar.bgMaxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of background agents that can run simultaneously

sidecar.promptPruning.enabled.markdownDescription:
Prune prompts before sending to paid backends (Anthropic, OpenAI). Collapses whitespace, truncates oversized tool results, and dedupes repeated file content. **Safe for agent loops** — only lossy on tool output, never on user or assistant messages.

Check the savings in `SideCar: Show Session Spend` after each run.

sidecar.promptPruning.maxToolResultToken.markdownDescription: Maximum token count for any single `tool_result` block sent to a paid backend. Longer results are head+tail truncated with an elision marker. Raise this if you need the agent to see full file contents; lower it to reduce cost on heavy exploration tasks.

sidecar.delegateTask.enabled.markdownDescription: Expose the `delegate_task` tool to paid backends. The orchestrator can offload read-only research (file reads, greps, searches) to a local Ollama worker and receive a compact summary, cutting paid-model token usage on heavy exploration tasks. **No-op on local-only setups.**

sidecar.delegateTask.workerModel.markdownDescription: Ollama model used by the `delegate_task` worker. Empty string = reuse the chat model. Recommended: a code-tuned model like `qwen3-coder:30b` or `deepseek-coder:33b`.

sidecar.delegateTask.workerBaseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL of the Ollama instance the `delegate_task` worker connects to. Must point at a local or reachable Ollama server — never Anthropic or OpenAI (defeats the purpose).

sidecar.delegateTask.maxIterations.markdownDescription: Hard cap on agent iterations for a delegated worker run. Workers are scoped to focused read-only research, so a tight cap protects against runaway loops and unintended cost. Raise this only if your delegated tasks legitimately need more steps (e.g. deep multi-file analysis). The main agent loop is bounded separately by `#sidecar.agentMaxIterations#`.

sidecar.outboundAllowlist.markdownDescription:
Outbound host allowlist for URL fetching in chat.

When non-empty, SideCar only fetches URLs whose hostname matches one of these entries. Empty (the default) allows every public URL — SSRF and private-IP blocking still apply.

Patterns support a leading `*.` wildcard for subdomains:

- `github.com` — exact match only
- `*.github.com` — any subdomain (`api.github.com`, `raw.github.com`)

Pairs with the built-in exfiltration defense on `web_search`, which refuses to send queries containing credential-shaped tokens regardless of this setting.

sidecar.includeActiveFile.description: Include the currently active file in chat context

sidecar.completionModel.description: Model for inline completions (leave empty to use the chat model)

sidecar.completionMaxTokens.description: Maximum tokens for inline completion responses

sidecar.completionDebounceMs.description: Minimum interval in milliseconds between completion requests

sidecar.verboseMode.markdownDescription: Show detailed agent reasoning in the chat — iteration summaries, tool selection context, and system prompt assembly. Turn off for a quieter UI at the cost of debuggability.

sidecar.expandThinking.markdownDescription: Show model reasoning (`<thinking>`) blocks expanded by default instead of collapsed. Useful when watching how frontier models reason, noisy for everyday chat.

sidecar.enableMermaid.markdownDescription: Render fenced ` ```mermaid ` code blocks as diagrams in the chat. Disable to skip the 5.2MB mermaid.js load and render them as plain code — useful if you never ask for diagrams and want a smaller runtime footprint.

enumDescriptions: Tighter message padding and gaps — fits more messages on screen, good for side-by-side layouts.
The default — balanced padding matching VS Code's overall density.
Relaxed padding and larger gaps — easier reading on high-DPI displays.
sidecar.chatDensity.markdownDescription: Chat panel density. Affects message padding, avatar size, and vertical rhythm — not the font size (use `#sidecar.chatFontSize#` for that).

sidecar.chatFontSize.markdownDescription: Chat UI base font size in pixels. Range: 10–22.

sidecar.chatAccentColor.markdownDescription: Override the chat accent color used for user messages and step indicators. Any valid CSS color (`#ffbb00`, `hsl(200 80% 50%)`, `royalblue`). Leave empty to inherit from the active VS Code theme.

sidecar.terminalErrorInterception.description: Detect non-zero exit codes in the integrated terminal and offer to diagnose the failure in the SideCar chat. Requires VS Code shell integration.

sidecar.jsDocSync.enabled.description: On save, check TypeScript / JavaScript files for JSDoc @param tags that no longer match the function signature. Stale tags are surfaced as diagnostics with quick-fix actions to add, remove, or rename them.

sidecar.readmeSync.enabled.description: On save and open, check fenced code blocks in README.md for calls to workspace-exported functions whose argument count no longer matches the current signature. Stale calls are surfaced as diagnostics with a quick-fix that rewrites the arguments.

sidecar.pinnedContext.description: Files or folders always included in context (relative paths). Use @pin:path in chat to add dynamically.

sidecar.autoFixOnFailure.description: Automatically check for errors after agent edits and feed them back to the model for self-correction

sidecar.completionGate.enabled.description: Require the agent to run lint and tests for files it edited before declaring completion. Catches cases where the agent reports a change as done without verifying.

sidecar.critic.enabled.description: Run an adversarial critic LLM call after every write_file / edit_file and after every failed run_tests. High-severity findings block the agent until addressed; low-severity findings surface as chat annotations only. Doubles API spend on edit turns — off by default.

sidecar.critic.model.description: Model to use for the adversarial critic call. Empty string (default) means reuse sidecar.model. Set to a cheaper / faster model (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5 when the main model is claude-opus-4-6) to cut cost while keeping the critic enabled.

sidecar.critic.blockOnHighSeverity.description: When the critic reports a high-severity finding, inject a synthetic user message forcing the agent to address it before the turn can finish. When false, high-severity findings surface as chat annotations only (same as low-severity).

sidecar.fetchUrlContext.markdownDescription: Automatically fetch web-page content when URLs are pasted into chat. The fetched HTML is converted to markdown and attached as context on the next turn.

sidecar.fallbackBaseUrl.markdownDescription:
Fallback backend URL. If the primary backend fails repeatedly, SideCar switches to this URL automatically. Leave empty to disable fallback.

Typical use: primary = Anthropic, fallback = local Ollama, so a transient API outage doesn't block your work.

sidecar.fallbackApiKey.markdownDescription: API key for the fallback backend. **Stored in VS Code SecretStorage** — plaintext values are migrated on activation and then cleared from `settings.json`.

sidecar.fallbackModel.markdownDescription: Model to use on the fallback backend. Empty string = reuse the primary model (only useful when the primary and fallback are the same provider family).

sidecar.dailyBudget.markdownDescription: Daily spending budget in USD for paid backends. Agent runs are blocked when the limit is reached. `0` = disabled. Check current spend with `SideCar: Show Session Spend`.

sidecar.weeklyBudget.markdownDescription: Weekly spending budget in USD for paid backends. Agent runs are blocked when the limit is reached. `0` = disabled.

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with SideCar
walkthroughs.description: Local-first AI coding assistant — chat, inline edits, code actions, and native Problems integration.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.description: What SideCar is and what ships in the box.

steps.title: Pick a backend
steps.description: Run locally with Ollama, or plug in an Anthropic / OpenAI key. Keys are stored in SecretStorage.

steps.title: Open the chat
steps.description: Toggle the SideCar chat sidebar and send your first message.

steps.title: Inline editing and the lightbulb
steps.description: Inline chat with ⌘I, and Fix / Explain / Refactor from the code actions menu.

steps.title: Discover every action
steps.description: Browse the Command Palette, Problems panel integration, and pending-change review.

scripts.copy-grammars: mkdir -p grammars && cp node_modules/web-tree-sitter/tree-sitter.wasm grammars/ && cp node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-typescript.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-tsx.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-javascript.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-python.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-rust.wasm node_modules/tree-sitter-wasms/out/tree-sitter-go.wasm grammars/
scripts.build: npm run compile && npm run bundle && npm run copy-grammars
scripts.check: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm run test
